free_text: Formation of tritium in nuclear reactions induced
by deuterons and alpha-particles.
(d,x) and (alpha,x) reactions
additional data
- (p,x) reaction
Stack of Al, V, Nb and Au foils (purity better than
/By author/.After the irradiation foils were heated
in hydrogen atmosphere at 950-1000 degree and the
activity of the extracted tritium was determined in
a proportional counter using methane as a counting
gas. The tritium activity was measured several days
after irradiations, so short-lived activities were
no longer present.
No corrections were applied for the losses of tritium
recoiling out of the front foils of the stack backwards
with respect to the beam direction. However, from the
shape of the depth distributions it was estimated that
these losses amount in general only to very few percent
of the thick-target yields.
The standard deviations of the flux of the
projectiles that measured by current integration.
See MEMO CP-D/130.
Remarks of V. McLane are included.
Data units are changed
Corrections were made according to N.Otsuka
and A.Nichols remarks.
SD: SF5=IND deleted from REACTION code in
Subents 002-026 (no precursor exists - remark from
N.Otsuka). Small corrections in all Subents. ERR-ANALYS
update in all Subents.
SD: Institute code was corrected.
SD: ref. RCA,29,173 added.

SD: SF5=IND deleted from REACTION code.
PRT/INC -> PRD/INC in DATA section.
Mean cross sections for the formation of tritium,
averaged over the respective energy interval, were
obtained from the differences between the respective
thick-target yields divided by the number of target
atoms per cm2 contained in that layer of the stack in
which the energy of the projectiles is degraded from
the upper to the lower limit of the energy interval.
For these calculations the range energy tables of
Williamson (see REL-REF) were used.
The error of the mean cross sections
was estimated to be in general smaller than 20%.
However, the uncertainty for the lowest energy
intervals is larger due to the uncertainty of the
threshold energies of the tritium producing reactions.
SD: SF8=AV, SF9=DERIV added, SF5=IND
deleted from REACTION code.
